headline: Esports Market Set To Surpass $5 Billion By 2029: What Investors Need To Know
summary: Discover how the esports market is set to exceed $5 billion by 2029, with expert advice on trends, challenges, and investment opportunities.

headline: Musk Ally Ramaswamy Takes Aim At Biden's $6.6B Loan To Tesla Rival Rivian
summary: Newly appointed government efficiency co-czar Vivek Ramaswamy says he will scrutinize a loan issued by the Biden administration to Tesla rival Rivian.

Deal Dispatch: Stoli On The Rocks As US Business Goes Bankrupt, Ally Financial To Sell Credit Card Unit

summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors’ favorite stocks over the last week — here’s a look at some of our top stories.

headline: MicroStrategy's Imminent Inclusion In Nasdaq 100 Could Reportedly Spark Up To $2B In Fund Inflows
summary: MicroStrategy looks set to join the NASDAQ 100 index, prompting a major reconstitution of the index.

headline: Crypto Analyst Says This Altcoin Is Primed For A 70% Rally, Notes Bitcoin Could Cross $100K If This Happens
summary: Renowned crypto analyst Michaël van de Poppe has predicted a significant rally for a top altcoin, alongside Bitcoin’s (CRYPTO: BTC) potential historic surge.

headline: Amazon's Focus Shift To Black Friday From Cyber Monday Is Paying Off, Says Gene Munster: 'Small Changes To Profitability Can Have Measurable Impact To Earnings'
summary: Amazon's shifted of focus from Cyber Monday to Black Friday for the second year in a row and is reaping the benefits of this move, according to Deepwater Asset Management's Gene Munster.

headline: MicroStrategy's Michael Saylor Calls Bitcoin The 'Manhattan In Cyberspace,' Says It's An Asset You Could Expect To 'Last 1,000 Years'
summary: MicroStrategy co-founder Michael Saylor likened Bitcoin to "Manhattan in cyberspace," describing it as a long-lasting asset.

headline: OpenAI Faces Fresh Legal Battle As Canadian News Giants Sue ChatGPT-Maker Citing Violation Of Copyright Laws
summary: OpenAI is facing a landmark lawsuit in Canada from major news publishers, accusing the ChatGPT maker of violating copyright laws by using their content without authorization or compensation, amidst ongoing debates over AI's impact on journalism and intellectual property.

headline: Tesla Bull Gary Black Says Cybercab Won't See An Early Roll Out, Expects A Cheaper Compact Car Under $30K To Launch In First Half Of 2025
summary: Gary Black of The Future Fund LLC confirmed that Tesla's Cybercab won't launch until 2026, dismissing early rollout rumors, while highlighting the company's focus on a $30K EV slated for 2025.

headline: JPMorgan, Tesla Settle 3-Year-Old Dispute Over Stock Warrants
summary: JPMorgan has decided to withdraw its lawsuit against Tesla, which was centered around a breach of contract involving stock warrants from 2014.
